speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Tonight, this candle will give you your gift, mi vida. Strengthen our community, strengthen our home. Make your family proud.

speaker: YOUNG MIRABEL
text: Make my family proud. Mirabel nods, solemn but bursting with pride. Abuela beams. As fireworks explode, the magical House knocks a CLOCK toward them, nudging that it’s time to start the ceremony.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Yes, yes, Casita, we’re going. The House brings Mirabel her shoes. She giggles, then helps Abuela to the door, holding her hand, excited and nervous.

speaker: YOUNG MIRABEL
text: What do you think my gift will be? Abuela Alma bends down to Mirabel, full of love and pride.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: You are a wonder, Mirabel Madrigal. Whatever gift awaits, will be just as special as you. On Mirabel’s face, we match to her standing in the courtyard as the curtain opens on her GIFT CEREMONY. As Mirabel stares at her undulating door, full of wonder, its glittering magic blooms brighter and brighter, and we transition to our title:

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Make your family proud.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Morning, Abuelo.

speaker: PUMPED JUANCHO
text: Hey! When’s the magic gift happen?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: My cousin’s ceremony is tonight. She goes back to setting the table, but:

speaker: LITTLE ALEJANDRA
text: What’s his gift?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: We’re gonna find out.

speaker: PUMPED JUANCHO
text: What’s your gift?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Who’s asking?

speaker: KIDS
text: Us!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Well, “us,” I can’t just talk about myself... I’m only part of the amazing Madrigals.

speaker: KIDS
text: Who’s all the amazing Madrigals?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Aw, you’re not gonna leave me alone, are you? Casita, help me out. AND THE MUSIC KICKS IN!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Drawers. Floors. Doors -- Let’s goooo!
parenthetical: (House moves its drawers) | (House moves its floors) | (the magic doors glow) | (singing)

speaker: MIRABEL
text: THIS IS MY FAMILY,

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WHOA, BUT LET’S BE CLEAR, ABUELA

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WHOA, AND EVERY YEAR OUR FAMILY BLESSINGS GROW! THERE’S JUST A LOT YOU’VE SIMPLY GOT TO KNOW, SO!

speaker: KIDS
text: Oh my gosh, it’s them!/ What are the gifts?! / I can’t remember all the gifts/ Who is who?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Alright, alright, relax.

speaker: LITTLE ALEJANDRA
text: It is physically impossible to relax!

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: Tell us everything! What are your powers?

speaker: PUMPED JUANCHO
text: JUST TELL US WHAT EVERYONE CAN DO!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: And that’s why coffee’s for grownups. Mirabel takes Juancho’s coffee cup and steps up to a MURAL of the Madrigal family.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: MY TIO BRUNO --

speaker: CROWD
text: -- WE DON’T TALK ABOUT BRUNO --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: THEY SAY HE SAW THE FUTURE,

speaker: MIRABEL
text: OH, AND THAT’S MY MOM JULIETA, HERE’S HER DEAL. WHOA, THE TRUTH IS, SHE CAN HEAL

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WELCOME TO THE FAMILY MADRIGAL! THE HOME OF THE FAMILY MADRIGAL! HEY COMING THROUGH! I KNOW IT SOUNDS A BIT FANTASTICAL AND MAGICAL, BUT I’M PART OF THE FAMILY MADRIGAL! Mirabel points out her dad, Agustín, and Tio Félix.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: AND NOW THEY’RE PART OF THE FAMILY

speaker: KIDS
text: Who’s a sister, who’s a cousin?! / There’s so many people!/ How do you keep them all straight?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Okay, okay, okay, okay... SO MANY KIDS IN OUR HOUSE SO LET’S TURN THE SOUND UP! YOU KNOW WHY? I THINK IT’S TIME FOR A GRANDKID ROUNDUP!

speaker: MIRABEL/KIDS/TOWN
text: GRANDKID ROUNDUP!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: CAMILO SHAPE SHIFTS, ANTONIO GETS HIS GIFT TODAY! Pop to ANTONIO being dressed in his CEREMONY OUTFIT. We see ISABELA and LUISA .

speaker: MIRABEL
text: MY OLDER SISTERS, ISABELA AND LUISA... ONE STRONG, ONE GRACEFULPERFECT IN EVERY WAY! POP to Isabela, who Abuela fawns over.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: GROWS A FLOWER, THE TOWN GOES WILD. SHE’S THE PERFECT GOLDEN CHILD. As flowers go everywhere, we find the very handsome MARIANO, who catches some of Isabela’s flowers. Behind him, Luisa walks in and lifts... A BRIDGE.

speaker: TOWNSFOLK
text: LUISA, LUISA, LUISA, LUISA

speaker: MIRABEL
text: AND LUISA’S SUPER STRONG. THE BEAUTY AND THE BRAWN DO NO WROOONG!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Family, let’s get ready!

speaker: LUISA
text: Coming Abuela! BELLS in town ring and the family starts to head back home.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: THAT’S LIFE IN THE FAMILY MADRIGAL!

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: But what’s your gift? Mirabel looks caught... but before she can get out of there, someone throws her an accordion and the kids start pumping

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: BUT WHAT ABOUT MIRABEL?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: IT STARTS WITH ABUELA, AND THEN TIA PEPA, SHE HANDLES THE WEATHER

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: BUT WHAT ABOUT MIRABEL?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: MY MOM JULIETA, CAN MAKE YOU FEEL

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: BUT WHAT ABOUT MIRABEL?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: MY DAD AGUSTÍN, WELL, HE’S ACCIDENT

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: BUT WHAT ABOUT MIRABEL?

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: MIRABEL!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: MY PRIMO CAMILO WON’T STOP UNTIL HE MAKES YOU SMILE TODAY!

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: MIRABEL!

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: MIRABEL!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: LOOK, IT’S MISTER MARIANO, HEY YOU

speaker: PRIMA DONNA
text: YO, I’VE SAID TOO MUCH AND THANK YOU BUT I REALLY GOTTA GO!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: MY FAMILY’S AMAZING!

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: MIRABEL!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: AND I’M IN MY FAMILY, SO...

speaker: KIDS/TOWN
text: MIRABEL!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: MIRABEL!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: What are you doing...?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Uh, they were just asking about the family and --

speaker: LITTLE ALEJANDRA
text: She was about to tell us about her super awesome gift! Abuela looks confused, but DOLORES pops up out of nowhere:

speaker: DOLORES
text: Oh, Mirabel didn’t get one. Dolores thinking she was helpful and heads off. Mirabel, busted, looks back to Abuela, who moves on. The kids look back to Mirabel, feeling like they’ve been duped.

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: You didn’t get a gift? Mirabel opens her mouth to try to spin this bad news, but-- a guy approaches with a DONKEY laden with supplies.

speaker: DONKEY DELIVERY GUY
text: Mirabel? Delivery! I gave you the “special” since you’re the only Madrigal kid with no gift. I call it the “not special” special. Since uh, since you have no gift. Crickets. The kids just stare at Mirabel. A beat.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Thanks.

speaker: DONKEY DELIVERY GUY
text: Ooo and tell Antonio good luck. Last gift ceremony was a bummer. Last one being yours that did not work. He leaves. Innocent Cecilia looks at Mirabel.

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: If I was you, I’d be really sad.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Well, my little friend I am not, because the truth is, gift or no gift, I am just as special as the rest of my family. All around Mirabel her amazing family does amazing things. The kids look at her like she’s delusional.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Who wants more pink?

speaker: LUISA
text: Alright guys, where do I drop the wagon?

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: Maybe your gift is being in denial. Mirabel thinks about that as more flowers bloom and a rainbow appears over the house.

speaker: PEPA
text: I made a rainbow!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Oh sorry... excuse me...

speaker: ABUELA
text: Lift it higher... higher. CAMILO, watching from the balcony above, praises people.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Hey nice job over there, yeah yeah.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Camilo, we need another José. Camilo changes into a tall guy to help hang an “ANTONIO!” BANNER over a DOOR WITH A GLOWING BORDER AROUND IT.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Jooooosé!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Luisa, the piano goes upstairs.

speaker: LUISA
text: I’m on it. Careful, sis! Mirabel tries to balance the groceries as the wind picks up. Pepa is creating a TORNADO as she nervously paces.

speaker: PEPA
text: My baby’s night has to be perfect and it’s not perfect and people are going to be coming and nothing’s ready, the whole town will be here - FÉLIX Pepi, Pepi, Pepita - you’re tornadoing the flowers - the flowers!

speaker: ISABELA
text: Did someone say “flowers?” Isabela descends above Mirabel on a flowering vine and flowers bloom all around her like a goddamn angel. FÉLIX Our angel, our angel.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Please, don’t clap.

speaker: PEPA
text: Thank you.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Oh, it’s nothing. Isabela lands next to Mirabel, but doesn’t see her as she turns and bumps into her, covering both in flowers. As they walk side-by-side, we can feel their friction. Mirabel tries to get the petals off of her. Isabela makes herself perfect.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Uch, a little sisterly advice: if you weren’t always trying too hard you wouldn’t be in the way.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Actually, Isa, this is called helping, and I’m not in the way, you ar--oof-- Mirabel collides with a post, Isabela rolls her eyes.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Whoa. Mi vida, you okay? You don’t have to over-do it.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I know, Mamá, I just want to do my part like the rest of the family. Mirabel lifts a HEAVY OBJECT on the counter, the House

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Eee-- AGUSTÍN First gift ceremony since yours, lot of emotions - and I’ve been there --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Bee stings.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Ay, Agustín. Julieta sighs and starts working on a cure. AGUSTÍN -- when me and your Tio Félix married into the family - outsiders who had no gift, never ever would, surrounded by the exceptional, it was easy to feel... “un-ceptional”--

speaker: MIRABEL
text: -- okay, Papi -- AGUSTÍN -- I’m just saying I get it-- Julieta jams an AREPA in Agustín’s mouth, healing him and shoves him away.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Eat. Mi amor, if you ever want to talk --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I gotta put out the stuff, the house isn’t gonna decorate itself - Sorry, you could. You look great. The House raises its decorations back up. As Mirabel leaves with a bunch of stuff, Julieta looks on.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Corazon, remember you have nothing to prove. AGUSTÍN Yeah, remember... nothing to prove! Agustín gives Julieta a look like “we nailed it.” He sees that Julieta looks concerned and matches her concerned face.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: One hour! Mirabel startles and drops the candle, which ignite the candle doily she made for Abuela.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No, no no --

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Maybe you should leave the decorations to someone else? Mirabel peers up to see... ABUELA, watching Mirabel, who couldn’t look more incompetent as she stomps the fire.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No, I actually made these as a surprise - for you-- ooo-- As Mirabel holds up the doily, it smolders. Abuela sighs.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Mirabel, I know you want to help. But tonight must go perfectly. The whole town relies on our family, on our gifts. So the best way for... some of us to help is... to step aside, let the rest of the family do what they do best. Okay? Abuela looks at Mirabel with a tight-lipped smile, an awkward beat. Mirabel nods, wanting to be a team player.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Mm-hmm. Abuela looks at Mirabel, an awkward beat. Before anyone can say anything... a gust of wind blows and Abuela leaves.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Pepa, you have a cloud!

speaker: PEPA
text: I know, Mamá, but now I can’t find Antonio! What do you want from me?! As Pepa literally “storms off” Abuela steps away too. On her own again, Mirabel heads for her room, but overhears her mother talking to Abuela.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mamá, be nice to Mirabel okay. You know tonight will be hard for her.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: If the gift ceremony doesn’t go well this time, tonight will be hard for us all...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Everyone’s looking for you. This present will self-destruct if you don’t take it in three, two, one... TWO TINY HANDS pop out from under the bed, grab the present and disappear below. Smiling, Mirabel scootches under the bed and joins ANTONIO . Mirabel sees he’s anxious, plays it slow, treats him like a cohort instead of a little kid.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Nervous? You have nothing to worry about. You’re gonna get your gift and open that door... and it’s gonna be the coolest ever. I know it. Antonio thinks about this.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: What if it doesn’t work?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Well... in that impossible scenario... you’d stay in here in the nursery... with me. Forever. And I’d get you all to myself. Antonio looks at her, he clearly loves her a lot.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I wish you could have a door. Mirabel considers this for a moment, it clearly pains her but she puts on a brave face for Antonio.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You know what? You don’t have to worry about me, ‘cause I have an amazing family... and an amazing house, and an amazing you.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: And seeing you get your special gift and your door... that’s gonna make me way more happy than anything. She walks her fingers over to the present and pulls it close.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: But alas, I am going to miss having the world’s best roomie... She gestures to the present. Antonio opens it, revealing... a HAND-KNITTED JAGUAR in the style of Mirabel’s dress.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I know you’re an animal guy. And I made this, so when you move into your cool new room you always have something to snuggle with. The earnestness of the moment is gently cut as the House moves its floorboards to draw their attention to the time.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Alright, hombrecito, you ready? Antonio nods, ready. Mirabel moves to leave, but:

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Sorry, I gotta get one more squeeze. Mirabel sneaks another hug, but the House playfully pulls out them out from under the bed.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Okay, okay, we’re going! Ow!

speaker: LUISA
text: Here yah go.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Oye, Osvaldo! / Hola Señora. / Cecilia, up top! The HOUSE takes people’s hats as they head through the archway. LUISA walks by with a platter of food as kids climb on her. ISABELA showers guests with flower petals. Mariano admires her from afar. Antonio’s door catches the attention of two kids. They rush up the staircase which suddenly becomes a slide. The children laugh and run up it again.

speaker: PEPA
text: Look at you, all grown up -- As Pepa tears up, a cloud forms and starts to rain. FÉLIX Pepi, you’re gonna get him all wet. Camilo transforms himself into a tiny version of Félix.

speaker: CAMILO
text: You make-a your Papá proud. FÉLIX I don’t sound like that.

speaker: CAMILO
text: “I don’t sound like that.” Dolores tilts her head and listens to a sound we cannot hear.

speaker: DOLORES
text: Abuela says... it’s time. Pepa bends down to Antonio, kisses him.

speaker: PEPA
text: We’ll be waiting at your door! FÉLIX Okay, okay, vamo, vamo, vamo--

speaker: CAMILO
text: “Okay, okay, Vamo, vamo, vam--” -- - alright I’m done! As Félix yanks Camilo out, we focus on Mirabel watching Antonio. We can see Mirabel’s anxiety beginning to bubble. Drums play and we find Abuela standing in the courtyard, holding the MAGIC CANDLE. She is anxious as well.

speaker: ABUELA ALBA
text: Fifty years ago, in our darkest moment, this candle blessed us with a miracle. We find older members of the crowd nodding along. Over this, we also find Mirabel listening... the words triggering memories of her own gift day.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: And the greatest honor of our family has been to use our blessings to serve this beloved community. Tonight, we come together once more as another steps into the light... to make us proud. The crowd cheers and the “curtains” open to reveal Antonio. He freezes, too scared to move. The House encourages Antonio, but he won’t budge. Antonio turns to Mirabel, hand outstretched, asking for help. Mirabel looks to Abuela, whose rules for tonight are clear, then back to Antonio, torn.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I can’t...

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I need you. Antonio motions to Mirabel again. Mirabel wonders if she can do this... if she has the strength to step out and face the reminders of the worst night of her life. Then, as everyone watches, Mirabel steps out and takes Antonio’s hand. SHE IS GOING TO “WALK HIM DOWN THE AISLE.”

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Come on. Let’s get you to your door. The family, the town and Abuela see Mirabel join Antonio. We clock Abuela looking anxious. But Antonio holds Mirabel’s hand tightly, he needs her... and they begin to walk. With every step, it gets harder for Mirabel... the memory of walking to her own door still so fresh in her mind - we intercut with moments of her ceremony as a five-year-old. Mirabel looks to the MAGIC CANDLE in Abuela’s hands. As they climb the stairs to Antonio’s glowing door, every step is a painful reminder. We intercut with the moment Mirabel’s door vanished. This is the moment everyone’s expectations of Mirabel changed... when Abuela looked at her differently... when Mirabel looked at herself differently. But Mirabel pushes through the pain of those memories and helps Antonio reach his door, handing him off to Abuela.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Will you use your gift to honor our miracle? Will you serve this community and strengthen our home? Antonio nods and Alma gestures to the door. Will it work? Mirabel sees Antonio worry for just a moment, but then... as he touches the doorknob, he is FILLED WITH MAGIC! And... a TOUCAN lands on Antonio’s arm and chirps. Like it’s SAYING something to him. Antonio smiles, delighted.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Uh-huh, uh-huh, I understand you. Of course they can come! And then DOZENS OF ANIMALS/BIRDS flock to him... and his door FORMS INTO AN ANIMAL MENAGERIE. HE CAN COMMUNICATE WITH ANIMALS! Abuela is overjoyed and hugely relieved.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: WE HAVE A NEW GIFT!! People cheer and fireworks go off and...

speaker: LITTLE ALEJANDRA
text: It’s bigger on the inside?! But as the kids gawk, ROOAR! A JAGUAR races past and to Antonio’s delight, the jaguar scoops him up onto his back and races through the room - every kid’s dream come true. FÉLIX WEPA ANTONIO! Vaya, vaya. As the jaguars picks up speed, it playfully roars at Antonio.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: You wanna go where?! The Jaguar races UP a tree trunk, tossing Antonio in the air.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Wooooaaah! He bounces across hammocks of COATIS and skates on the surface of a RIVER, using a SNAKE as a rope, having the time of his life! Everyone watches as this introverted kid hollers with delight!

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Woooo-hoooo! The jaguar and Antonio stop near the family. Félix celebrates and the Jaguar leaps onto Agustín, nearly knocking him over. Mirabel watches, relieved for Antonio... but the moment turns as... Abuela hurries over to Antonio, proud and relieved and pulls him in for a hug, her eyes full of pride.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: I knew you could do it... a gift just as special as you. The words pierce Mirabel’s heart... avoiding her feelings is no longer possible. She’s so alone... and unworthy... watching her family she feels she is not truly a part of.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: We need a picture! Everyone, come come, come, come, come! It’s a great night, it’s a perfect night.

speaker: FAMILY / EVERYONE
text: LA FAMILIA MADRIGAL! As everyone cheers... POOF! The brilliant light of a magnesium flash, sends us into... SUPER SLOW MOTION. As we capture the family’s celebration, we push toward Mirabel... who watches in the shadows from the side, trying to keep it together... and failing...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: DON’T BE UPSET, OR MAD AT ALL...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: DON’T FEEL REGRET, OR SAD AT ALL...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: HEY, I’M STILL A PART OF THE FAMILY

speaker: MIRABEL
text: AND I’M FINE, I AM TOTALLY FINE

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I’M NOT FINE, I’M NOT FINE... As Mirabel faces her reality, she finally tells us how she really feels.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I CAN’T MOVE THE MOUNTAINS I CAN’T MAKE THE FLOWERS BLOOM I CAN’T TAKE ANOTHER NIGHT

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I CAN’T HEAL WHAT’S BROKEN CAN’T CONTROL THE MORNING RAIN OR A

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I WOULD HEAL WHAT’S BROKEN

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I AM READY! C’MON, I’M READY! I’VE BEEN PATIENT AND STEADFAST AND STEADY!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Am I too late for a miracle...? Mirabel stands in the lonely silence of the now empty courtyard, when... CLANK - a ROOF TILE FALLS into the courtyard next to her, breaking. Curious, Mirabel picks up the broken tile from the courtyard... as she inspects it, she CUTS HER HAND on its sharp broken edge.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Ow -- As she winces, she notices something on the courtyard tiles below her... a tile MALFUNCTIONING... fritzing in its place. Mirabel looks at it... confused. She reaches out her hand...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Casita...? Mirabel reaches toward the fritzing tiles when... CRACK! A small crack on the floor tiles begins to form. What the hell? Mirabel recoils, afraid. CRACK CRACK! The crack splinters. Growing! Around Mirabel and the entire courtyard. They spread, rippling everywhere! Up the stairs! As the tiles keep fritzing, Mirabel races after the growing cracks... which ripple past PEDRO’S PORTRAIT to the second floor. Mirabel loses the path for a moment, but hears cracks down the hall. She looks just in time to see the cracks pass ISABELA’S DOOR, nearly extinguishing its magic. Mirabel moves faster, following the disturbing cracks past Luisa’s door... Isabela’s door... and continue toward... ABUELA’S DOOR and... the MAGIC CANDLE! As the cracks swirl and multiply, the candle grows dimmer and dimmer! Mirabel watches in horror, it’s like the whole house grows dark and as the cracks expand around Mirabel, we push

speaker: MIRABEL
text: THE HOUSE IS IN DANGER! THE HOUSE IS IN DANGER! Everyone stops and looks at her, the band stops their music. Abuela stares at Mirabel, concerned.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: The tiles were falling and there were cracks everywhere and... the candle almost went out. People in the party begin to murmur uncomfortably. Abuela clocks this and looks to Mirabel.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Show me.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: What? No, that’s --- the cracks were there, they were... everywhere. The house was in trouble, the, the candle was... Isabela looks at Mirabel and scoffs. Abuela looks at the candle, then back to Mirabel, embarrassed and disappointed.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Abuela, I promise -

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: That’s enough. As nervous whispers build within the crowd, Abuela gives a pointed look to Mirabel, then puts on her “face of confidence” and turns to address the crowd.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: There is nothing wrong with La Casa Madrigal. The magic is strong... and so are the drinks! Please -- music! A bailar, a bailar! Agustín, realizing how awkward this is for Mirabel, motions to Luisa, who brings him a PIANO, which he plays to smoothover the weirdness. Isabela looks at Mirabel and scoffs. As people head back into the party, Julieta approaches Mirabel, concerned.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: If it was all in my head, how did I cut my hand? I would never ruin Antonio’s night. Is that really what you think?

speaker: JULIETA
text: What I think is that today was very hard for youMIRABEL That’s - I was looking out for the family. And I might not be super-strong like Luisa, or effortlessly perfect like “Señorita Perfecta” Isabela, who’s never even had a bad hair day. But... Whatever... The House delivers an arepa to Julieta, who moves to Mirabel.

speaker: JULIETA
text: I wish you could see yourself the way I do. You are perfect, just like this. You’re just as special as anyone else in this family. Mirabel hears the words... but before they can land, Julieta holds Mirabel’s hand and heals her.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Mm-hm. You just healed my hand with an arepa con queso.

speaker: JULIETA
text: I healed your hand... ...with my love for my daughter, with her wonderful brain-- Mirabel tries to wriggles away from Julieta’s affection.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Agh.

speaker: JULIETA
text: --big heart--

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Stop.

speaker: JULIETA
text: --cool glasses--

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Mamá. Julieta gives Mirabel a big embarrassing kiss on the cheek.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Ay, te amo, cosa linda. But though Julieta tries to snuggle up to Mirabel, Mirabel is not won over, and extricates herself.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I know what I saw. Julieta sighs.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mira, my brother Bruno lost his way in this family... I don’t want the same for you. Get some sleep. You’ll feel better tomorrow.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Ay, Pedro... I need you. Cracks in our casita... If our family knew how vulnerable we truly are... if our miracle is dying. We cannot lose our home again. Tears are welling up in Abuela’s eyes now.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Why is this happening? Open my eyes. If the answer is here, help me find it. Help me protect our family. Help me save... our miracle. Abuela turns and heads back into her room. We push in on Mirabel, if there was any doubt that she needed to act, it’s gone. The cracks were real, she’s not crazy, she needs to help Abuela, she needs to save the magic, save the family. Mirabel stares at the candle.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I will save the miracle.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wait. How do I save a miracle? The House pantomimes back, it has no idea. Mirabel thinks for a moment.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I figure out what’s happening to the miracle. Oh, I have no idea, but there is one person in this family who hears everything about everything... Mirabel looks at a picture of her family, and zeroes in on... DOLORES, her cousin who hears everything.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: ....so if anyone would know what’s wrong with the magic, it’s her. Several AREPAS are picked up by... DOLORES, who is stacking her plate with food. She looks oddly nervous. Behind her, Mirabel enters and locks on target.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Dolores, hey. You know, out of all my older cousins, you’re like my favorite cousin, so I feel like I can talk to you about anything, ergo you can talk to me about anything, like the problem with the magic last night that no one seemed to worry about, but maybe you heard about that maybe I should know about. Behind them is FÉLIX, who stands with THE REAL DOLORES. FÉLIX Camilo! Stop pretending you’re Dolores so you can have seconds. The “DOLORES” near Mirabel, morphs back into “Camilo.” The House takes his food back and whacks his hand.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Worth a shot. Ah! Ey! The REAL DOLORES whispers in Mirabel’s ear.

speaker: DOLORES
text: The only one worried about the magic is you... and the rats talking in the walls... oh, and Luisa. I heard her eye twitching all night. A lightbulb goes off in Mirabel’s head. She looks over at Luisa, who is carrying a table over with one hand.

speaker: LUISA
text: There we go. But as Mirabel heads for Luisa, Abuela Alma enters.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Everyone to the table. Let’s go, let’s go -- Mirabel takes Pepa’s seat so that she can sit next to Luisa.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Luisa--

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Family, we are all thankful for Antonio’s wonderful new... gift... Abuela goes to her chair, but finds COATIS in it.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I told ‘em to warm up your seat. Antonio smiles to Abuela, who manages a smile back.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Thank you, Toñito. I’m sure today we’ll find a way to put your blessings to good use.... ...as must we all, we are the caretakers of this Encanto... As Abuela continues, Mirabel leans over to Luisa.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Luisa, Dolores says you’re totally freaking out, any chance you maybe know something about last night, with the magic - You do! Mirabel pounds the table, excited.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Mirabel. Mirabel looks up, busted by Abuela, for talking while Abuela was giving a speech.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: If you can’t pay attention I will help you.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Actually, I--

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Casita? The House MOVES Mirabel next to Abuela, away from Luisa.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: As I was saying, we must never take our miracle for granted, so today, we will work twice as hard.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Mhmm, I will help Luisa -- Mirabel stands to go back to Luisa, but Abuela continues.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Stop. First, an announcement. I’ve spoken to the Guzmans about Mariano’s proposal to Isabela. Dolores, do we have a date?

speaker: DOLORES
text: Tonight. He wants five babies. Isabela nervously sprouts some flowers.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Wonderful, such a fine young man with our perfect Isabela will bring a new generation of magical blessings and make both of our families stronger. Camilo morphs into a snarky version of MARIANO, Isabela swats him with flowers to get him to stop.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Mmmm. Ay!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Okay, our community is counting on us, La familia Madrigal!

speaker: MIRABEL/ FAMILY
text: -- La Familia Madrigal!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Luisa hey -- As everyone gets up, Mirabel sees Luisa is gone. Mirabel

speaker: ISABELA
text: What is your problem?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Ugh, sorry - - Hey Luisa! Hold up!

speaker: SRA. OSMA
text: Luisa, can you re-route the river?

speaker: LUISA
text: Will do!

speaker: SR. RENDON
text: Luisa, the donkeys got out again.

speaker: LUISA
text: On it. As Luisa picks up a COUPLE OF DONKEYS, Mirabel awkwardly climbs over the stone wall behind her.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Luisa, wait a second -- Luisa looks back, sees Mirabel, moves faster.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You gotta tell me about the magic. What’s going on? What are you hiding?

speaker: LUISA
text: Nothing, just gotta lotta chores, so maybe you should just go home.

speaker: SR. FLORES
text: Luisa, my house is leaning to the-- oh! Luisa knocks a crooked house straight and keeps walking.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Dolores said your eye was twitching and it never twitches, something’s making you nervous --

speaker: LUISA
text: Hey move, you’re gonna make me drop a donkey.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Luisa?! Will you just-- Just tell me what it is!

speaker: LUISA
text: There’s nothing to tell --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You’re obviously worried about something! Luisa, if you know what’s hurting the magic and it gets worse ‘cause you won’t tell me what’s wrong --!

speaker: LUISA
text: NOTHING’S WRONG! The DONKEYS are like, “YIKES.” Mirabel’s like “whoa.” Luisa realizes, tries to play it off.

speaker: LUISA
text: Wow, uh, sorry, that, uh, that snuck out there... what I meant was... “why would anything be wrong?” I’m totally fine. The magic’s fine. Luisa’s fine. I’m totally not nervous. As Luisa smiles, trying to be casual, her eye twitches.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Your eye is... doing the thing... Unwilling to admit there’s a problem, Luisa gets in Mirabel’s face.

speaker: LUISA
text: I’M THE STRONG ONE, I’M NOT NERVOUS, I’M AS TOUGH AS THE CRUST OF THE

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Okay...

speaker: LUISA
text: I MOVE MOUNTAINS, I MOVE CHURCHES, AND I GLOW, CUZ I KNOW WHAT MY

speaker: LUISA
text: I DON’T ASK HOW, HARD THE WORK IS, GOT A ROUGH, INDESTRUCTIBLE

speaker: LUISA
text: GIVE IT TO YOUR SISTER, YOUR SISTER’S OLDER

speaker: LUISA
text: GIVE IT TO YOUR SISTER, YOUR SISTER’S STRONGER

speaker: LUISA
text: YOU TRY TO STOP IT TOPPLIN’ BUT ON

speaker: LUISA
text: BUT WAIT, IF I COULD SHAKE

speaker: LUISA
text: GIVE IT TO YOUR SISTER, IT DOESN’T

speaker: LUISA
text: NO MISTAKES!

speaker: LUISA
text: GIVE IT TO YOUR SISTER, AND NEVER

speaker: LUISA
text: WHO AM I IF I DON’T HAVE WHAT IT

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I think you’re carrying way too much. Emotional, Luisa picks Mirabel up and hugs her too hard.

speaker: LUISA
text: Maybe I over do it.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Yup.

speaker: LUISA
text: Um, there is something you should know: last night, when you saw the cracks, I felt... weak. Luisa finally lets Mirabel breathe.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wha- What, what, what?!

speaker: SR. RENDON
text: Luisa, the donkeys!

speaker: LUISA
text: On it!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wait, wait, wait - what do you mean? What do you think’s hurting the magic? Luisa picks up a donkey, she has to hurry, she pulls Mirabel close to tell her a secret.

speaker: LUISA
text: Don’t know. But I heard the grown ups once: before Tio Bruno left, he had like some terrible vision about it.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Tio Bruno? What was in his vision?

speaker: LUISA
text: No one knows, they never found it. But if something’s wrong with the magic, start with Bruno’s tower, find that vision. Luisa heads off, carrying more donkeys.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wait, how do you “find” a vision?! What am I even looking for?

speaker: LUISA
text: If you find it, you’ll know. But be careful... that place is off limits for a reason. Mirabel turns back to the House... to Bruno’s tower.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Such a perfect match.

speaker: ISABELA
text: So perfect.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: And so good for the Encanto. Mirabel sneaks past the two and reaches Bruno’s door. No one’s been in there for years.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Casita, can you turn off the sand? Nothing happens. Mirabel looks back where she can see the floor tiles at the doorjamb say “the house cannot help beyond this point.” The gravity of this hits Mirabel.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You can’t help in here? The House responds “no... and I’m worried about you,” after all, Mirabel has never been “without the House.”

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I’ll be fine. I need to do this, for you, for Abuela... maybe a little for me. Find the vision, save the mir-ack! As Mirabel steps through the “sandfall” she...falls!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Whoa... Widen to reveal a SIGN reading “Your Future Awaits,” which points to the TOP of the cavern. HUNDREDS OF STAIRS lead all the way up. As Mirabel looks up - FLIT-FLIT-FLIT... Antonio’s TOUCAN flaps down next to her. It smiles and squawks “hello.”

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Oh, hello. Lotta stairs, but at least I’ll have a friend-- no, you flew away immediately. Mirabel watches it fly to the top. She’s on her own. Mirabel grits her teeth and starts climbing.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Alright. As she goes, we pop forward in time, and with each little pop, she gets more tired and is soon wheezing and struggling.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Welcome to the Family Madrigal...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: ...there’s so many stairs in the Casa Madrigal... you would think there’d be another way to get so high cause we’re

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Come on... As Mirabel takes a breath... then removes a rope railing on and throws it over a boulder above as an anchor.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Okay, I can do this. Ooo... Mirabel goes to swing across, slips, loses her balance, but... somehow makes it across. Stunned she succeeded, Mirabel pumps her fists --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wooooo! But as Mirabel stomps her foot to celebrate, the ledge below her cracks and plummets to the ground, nearly taking her with it. She looks to the toucan, who looks at her, judgmental. Deciding celebration time is over, Mirabel turns to the entrance of Bruno’s “temple.”

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Quitter.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Empty.... The wind moving through the cavern sucks the door shut, plunging Mirabel into DARKNESS. Mirabel panics, stuck in the darkness... but then... a light. Something glowing green... she’s standing on it... the sand itself seems to be glowing... Mirabel digs into the sand and finds... A GLOWING SHARD - LIKE A PIECE OF AN EMERALD RELIEF SCULPTURE.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: What’s hurting the magic?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Me...? RUMBLE! The entire cavern begins to shake, falling apart. Sand begins pouring in, covering the remaining shards under the sand. Panicking, Mirabel tries to dig out the remaining shards and shove them into her MOCHILA bag as the cavern quakes and CRACKS snake around the room. As pieces of stone and sand threaten to block the entrance... Mirabel races to collect one last piece, but as she dives to the door, it’s locked! Mirabel bangs at it desperately and then... jiggles the handle. Whoosh! A tidal wave of sand pushes her out to safety, almost sending her over the cliff. Mirabel looks down at the shards... what does it mean?

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Uch, where are you coming from in such a hurry?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I’m sorry. I was --

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: What’s in your hair?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Uh -- Is Mirabel busted? Just as Abuela begins to look suspicious... CRYING INTERRUPTS THE MOMENT! It’s the LOUD SOBBING of... LUISA, who staggers up the stairs behind them.

speaker: LUISA
text: My gift! I’m losing my gift!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: What?!

speaker: LUISA
text: Mirabel and I were having this little talk about me carrying too much, so I tried not to carry so much but I realized it was putting me behind and I knew I was gonna let everyone down and felt really bad so I was grabbing all the donkeys but then when I went to throw the donkeys in the barn, they were... heavy. Luisa runs off crying. Abuela looks to Mirabel, concerned.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: What did you do? What did you say to her?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Nothing. I - I don’t--

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Mirabel --

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Ach, I have to go get the Guzmans for Isabela’s engagement. Stay away from Luisa until I can talk to her. Tonight we can’t have any more problems. And whatever you’re doing, stop doing it! Abuela heads off. Mirabel looks at Luisa’s door which... flickers ever so slightly.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Why am I in your vision, Bruno? A FLASH of LIGHTNING and THUNDER shake the room. Startled, Mirabel turns to see PEPA, frazzled, in her doorway.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Tía, jeez --

speaker: PEPA
text: Sorry, sorry, I- I didn’t mean to --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Uh, Tía Pepa - if Bru- if he had a vision about “someone” what would it mean for them?

speaker: PEPA
text: We don’t talk about Bruno.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I know, it’s just, hypothetically, if he “saw” you --

speaker: PEPA
text: -- Mirabel, please. We need to get ready for the Guzmans.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I just want to know if it was generally positive... or... like less positive or-- Félix barges in! FÉLIX It was a nightmare!

speaker: PEPA
text: Félix! FÉLIX Ey, she needs to know, Pepi. She needs to know.

speaker: PEPA
text: We don’t talk about Bruno. FÉLIX He would see something terrible, and then crack-ba-boom, it would happen.

speaker: PEPA
text: We don’t talk about Bruno.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: What if you didn’t understand what he saw? FÉLIX Then you better figure it out, because it was coming for you!

speaker: PEPA
text: WE DON’T TALK ABOUT BRUNO, NO NO NO WE DON’T TALK ABOUT BRUNO... Pepa tries to get them to stop... but looks at Félix, who motions for Pepa to spill it, and suddenly... we are BACK IN THE PAST, and witnessing the day of their wedding.

speaker: PEPA
text: BUT! IT WAS MY WEDDING DAY FÉLIX

speaker: PEPA
text: YOU TELLING THIS STORY OR AM I? FÉLIX I’M SORRY MI VIDA GO ON...

speaker: PEPA
text: BRUNO SAYS, “IT LOOKS LIKE RAIN.” FÉLIX WHY DID HE TELL US?

speaker: PEPA
text: IN DOING SO, HE FLOODS MY BRAIN FÉLIX ABUELA, GET THE UMBRELLAS...

speaker: DOLORES
text: HEY - GREW TO LIVE IN FEAR OF BRUNO

speaker: DOLORES
text: IT’S A HEAVY LIFT WITH A GIFT SO HUMBLING, ALWAYS LEFT ABUELA AND THE FAMILY FUMBLING, GRAPPLING WITH PROPHECIES THEY COULDN’T UNDERSTAND, DO YOU UNDERSTAND? Mirabel gets pulled into a creepy underground space with Camilo, who shape-shifts to add a dramatic effect to his extra creepy Bruno tale, where he BECOMES Bruno himself.

speaker: DOLORES/CAMILO
text: WE DON’T TALK ABOUT BRUNO, NO NO NO DOLORES/CAMILO/FÉLIX/PEPA WE DON’T TALK ABOUT BRUNO Mirabel emerges from the cavernous underground, to find herself in the town plaza, surrounded by townsfolk who had bad run-ins with Bruno.

speaker: OSVALDO OROZCO ORTIZ
text: HE TOLD ME I’D GROW A GUT! AND JUST

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: OYE, MARIANO’S ON HIS WAY Dolores, on the balcony, hears her approaching with Mariano.

speaker: ISABELA
text: HEY, SIS, TONIGHT I WANT NOT A

speaker: DOLORES
text: IT’S LIKE I HEAR HIM NOW, I CAN HEAR HIM NOW, I CAN HEAR HIM NOW! MIRABEL leaves to assemble the vision.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: UM, BRUNO. YEAH ABOUT THAT BRUNO.

speaker: CAMILO
text: ISABELA, YOUR BOYFRIEND’S HERE

speaker: FAMILY
text: TIME FOR DINNER! The family prepares for the arrival of MARIANO - who parades up the street with a WEDDING RING. PEPA/FÉLIX

speaker: FAMILY
text: HE’S HERE! Mirabel hurries into her room and struggles to finish assembling the vision which glows brighter and brighter. ALL DON’T TALK ABOUT BRUNO!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WHY DID I TALK ABOUT BRUNO?! ALL NOT A WORD ABOUT BRUNO!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I NEVER SHOULDA BROUGHT UP BRUNO! SONG ENDS. Mirabel stares at the completed VISION image which shows... MIRABEL in front of her cracked house!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I... broke into Bruno’s tower, I found his last vision, the family’s in trouble, the magic is dying, the house is breaking, Luisa’s gift is fading and I think it’s all because of... me? Agustín stares at Mirabel, stunned.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Pa? Agustin eyes go wider... trying to formulate a plan. Agustín then shoves the vision into his POCKETS. AGUSTÍN We say nothing, Abuela wants tonight to be perfect, ‘til the Guzmans leave, you did not break into Bruno’s tower, the magic is not dying, the house is not breaking, Luisa’s gift is not fading, no one will know, just act normal, no one has to know. Suddenly there’s a NOISE, and they turn to see... DOLORES all the way across the courtyard... but her eyes tell us, she has clearly heard everything that Agustín just said.

speaker: DOLORES
text: I know.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: She’s gonna tell everyone.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Time to eat! Agustín realizes they’re busted. AGUSTÍN Miércoles.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: The Guzmans and the Madrigals together will be so good for the

speaker: ABUELA GUZMAN
text: Yes, then let’s hope tonight is not a horrible disaster.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: To a perfect night. Salud!

speaker: EVERYONE
text: Salud! Mirabel does her best to act normal as she maintains unwavering eye contact with Dolores, who struggles to keep the secret in... but looks like she’s gonna pop any minute. As the Abuelas lightly chuckle Mirabel keeps staring at Dolores, willing her not to divulge the secret.

speaker: ABUELA GUZMAN
text: I assumed that all was well in La Casa Madrigal...though, when it comes to my Mariano, it is always best to see for myself. A BOWL OF AVOCADO PASSES IN FRONT OF MIRABEL, blocking her view of Dolores.

speaker: MARIANO
text: Avocado? As the avocados pass, Mirabel now sees that Dolores couldn’t hold out any longer and is now telling Camilo the secret - he chokes on his food, his head changes into Mirabel then Bruno as he snarfs on the table.

speaker: ABUELA GUZMAN
text: La Familia Madrigal has always been a pillar of our communi...ty... Not great. Everyone looks at him - especially the two Abuelas . Abuela Alma doesn’t like this at all and pours more wine for Abuela Guzman to distract her. FÉLIX Camilo, fix your face. Camilo looks to Mirabel, who is starting to panic that Camilo now knows her secret too, but as she tries to “stare him into keeping quiet” a PITCHER of water PASSES IN FRONT OF HER.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Water? As the water jug passes, Mirabel sees that Camilo is now whispering to Félix. Félix’s eyes go wide and he chokes on some water, which flies onto Abuela Guzman’s plate. Abuela Alma watches this, quietly panicking, trying to maintain her composure.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Mirabel, the cream, please. Mirabel smiles at Abuela and turns to Agustín for the cream.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Pa, the cream. Reveal Agustín next to Mirabel. He tries to hand her the cream, but his hand is shaking like crazy. Mirabel takes the cream and passes it to Abuela. Thunder draws Mirabel’s attention to... Pepa. Félix is whispering in her ear, and now a small swirling hurricane forms over the dining room table.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Pepa? The cloud. Pepa tries her best to calm herself down to shrink the cloud.

speaker: PEPA
text: Clear skies, clear skies, clear skies.. Julieta leans over to Pepa, concerned. Pepa whispers in Julieta’s ear. Julieta flushes, then looks to Mirabel, DEEPLY

speaker: MARIANO
text: Mirabel...? Mirabel, freaked out, sits up fast, bonking her head on the underside of the table, then pops up.

speaker: MARIANO
text: ...everything okay? Mirabel stares at Mariano, caught. Then...

speaker: AGUSTIN
text: Everything’s great, she’s just... excited! For you to propose, which you should do as fast as you can --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Yes!

speaker: MARIANO
text: Uh, I was actually... gonna...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You were actually gonna! Great! Mirabel swivels Isabela’s chair toward Mariano. Abuela Guzman is a little weirded out.

speaker: LUISA
text: Okay.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Uh, it’s actually family tradition to sing after. On “AFTER” Mirabel shoves Mariano down on one knee. And motions for him to begin.

speaker: MARIANO
text: Isabela, most graceful of all the MadrigalsENCANTO 55. As the cracks spread, the House starts to malfunction. Mirabel moves behind Mariano, shielding the family from seeing the cracks, but now awkwardly horning in on the proposal.

speaker: MARIANO
text: Um...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You’re doing great. Under Agustín, Mirabel sees the Coatis take the GLOWING SHARDS of MIRABEL’S VISION OUT OF HIS POCKET.

speaker: MARIANO
text: The most perfect flower in this entire Encanto. Outside, Luisa tries to shove a piano, making horrible scraping noises. Abuela Guzman looks utterly confused.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No --

speaker: MARIANO
text: ...in this entire Encanto. The COATIS are almost done assembling the vision. Mirabel sees them and panics.

speaker: MARIANO
text: WILL YOU... MARRY ME?! No longer able to contain herself, Mirabel lunges at Coatis.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Nooo! Luisa falls to the ground, CRYING in defeat. Toucan is frightened by Pepa’s storm cloud and goes flying over Abuela Guzman’s head who ducks just in time.

speaker: ABUELA GUZMAN
text: What is happening?!

speaker: DOLORES
text: Mirabel found Bruno’s vision, she’s in it, she’s gonna destroy the magic and now we’re all doomed! AND ON CUE, THE COATIS pull the VISION up on to the table. As Mirabel tries to snatch it, she accidentally sends it sliding down the table, stopping right in front of Abuela Alma. EVERYONE LOOKS AT THE VISION, THEN TO MIRABEL and... CRACK! CRACK! CRACK! Cracks ripple seemingly from Mirabel around the whole room and everyone’s powers fritz and spasm. The ground cracks under Isabela who shoots a bunch of vines directly into Mariano’s face. Pepa’s storm cloud opens up, unleashing a torrent of water onto the whole table. As animals race out, they blow open the windows where we can see the WHOLE TOWN out front, ready to celebrate.

speaker: TOWNSFOLK
text: FELICIDADES - whoa...

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Señora, por favor!

speaker: ISABELA
text: I hate you! Luisa races past Mirabel, sobbing.

speaker: LUISA
text: Ahh, I’m a loser!

speaker: AGUSTIN
text: Luisa! Pepa runs out, her weather going crazy.

speaker: PEPA
text: What did you do?! More cracks ripple through the house.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I’m not doing anything! It’s Bruno’s vision! It’s... As Mirabel says that she notices... a green light moving on the ground... It’s... a shard of the vision... being carried by... A RAT. There are dozens of rats, all of them are taking the shards from the dining room and racing up to the balcony. Abuela chases after Abuela Guzman and Mariano.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: The magic is strong! EVERYTHING IS FINE! We are the Madrigals! MIRABEL! Thunder CLAPS!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: HEY!

speaker: CAMILO
text: It’s okay, Mami, deep breath in, deep breath out... WHAM! Something slams the wall next to them, and when Pepa hears the impact she panics, zapping Camilo with lightning.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Stop! STOP! Mirabel is almost to Bruno, but he jumps a CHASM and it looks too far for Mirabel to follow. As Bruno disappears down a distant corridor, Mirabel summons her courage and jumps and... MAKES IT! But the floor below her gives way and she falls, barely catching herself at the last second... but only holding on by a finger. Below is a seemingly endless fall into DARKNESS.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No-no-- HELP! HELP! CASITA?! CASITA?! HELP ME! HELP ME!
parenthetical: (desperate, to the House) | (but it can’t help)

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: You’re very sweaty. But before Mirabel can respond... the floor underneath Bruno collapses too, Mirabel grabs onto a loose board and catches Bruno. They are now both dangling from the pit. As Mirabel

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Oh no! But... there’s actually floor right beneath, he’s fine, they just couldn’t see it. Bruno looks around, surprised.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Huh. Tio Bruno climbs up the wall boards as Mirabel helps him up. Bruno looks to Mirabel... a long beat, then:

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Bye.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Knock, knock, knock. Knock on wood. Then he crosses his fingers and holds his breath.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Tio Bruno? He passes another piece of wood.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Knock, knock, knock. Knock on wood. Bruno uncrosses his fingers.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: You were never supposed to see that

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Sugar. And keeps walking past rows of HANGING ALOE PLANTS, then does a hop skip and a jump over a series of cracks on the ground.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Sana sana, colita de rana. Mirabel is about to ask a follow-up when they pass an area with TONS of cracks which have been painstakingly patched up.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wait, have you been in here... patching the cracks? Bruno looks up at all of the patched cracks.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Oh that? No, no, no, no, no, I’m too scared to go near those things. All the patching’s done by Hernando.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Who is... Hernan--

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: --I’m Hernando and I’m scared of nothing. It’s actually me. I used to say my real gift was acting. I’m Jorge, I make the spackle. Mirabel stares at Bruno.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: How long have you been... back here?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You never left.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Well, I- I left my tower, which was you know, a lotta stairs, and uh in here - eh? Kitchen adjacent - ooo plus, free entertainment!

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: So whattya like? Whattya like? You like sports? Game Show? Telenovelas? Their love could never be.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I don’t understand.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Well, because she’s his aunt and she has amnesia, so she can’t remember that she’s his aunt, you see it’s like a very forbidden kind ofMIRABEL I don’t understand why you “left,” but didn’t “leave?” Bruno looks a little uncomfortable about the question.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Oh, well... because you know the mountains around the Encanto are pretty tall... and uh - you know like I said, free food... and everything... Yeah, you guys, you guys love the free food, don’t ya? Always hungry, never satisfied... Mirabel notices a shaft of light... she follows it and peers through to discover the family DINING ROOM on the other side of the wall. She looks down to see Bruno has recreated his own version of the family table in his little room, complete with a stenciled plate. Mirabel looks back to Bruno, realizing how badly this guy still wants to be part of the family. Embarrassed, he avoids eye contact.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: My gift wasn’t helping the family... but uh... but I love my family, you know... I just don’t know how to... I just don’t know how to... On that last phrase, Bruno finally looks to Mirabel. She realizes how similar they are. She’s emotional, because she always felt alone. And inferior. Bruno shifts awkwardly.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Well anyway, I think you should go,

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Why was I in your vision? Tio Bruno...? I just... wanted to make the family proud of me... just once. But if I should stop... if I’m hurting my family... just tell me. He looks at her... debating revealing his deep secret.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I can’t tell you... Because I don’t know. That grabs Mirabel’s attention. Bruno takes the shards from his pocket and starts to assemble them...

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I had this vision the night that you didn’t get your gift. Abuela worried about the magic, so she begged me to look into the future, see what it meant...

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: And I saw the magic in danger... our house... breaking... and then... and then, and then... I saw you. But the vision was different. It... it would change... and there was no one answer... no clear fate. Like your future was undecided. But I knew how it was gonna look, I knew what everyone would think because I’m Bruno and everyone always assumes the worst... so... so... WE SEE BRUNO SMASH THE VISION... and LEAVE. Back in the Bruno’s room, Mirabel manipulates the vision, seeing it transform from cracks spreading to cracks disappearing.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You left... to protect me...?

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I don’t know which way it’ll go... but my guess... the family, the itself - it’s all gonna come down to you. This is a big moment for Mirabel... a lot to process. She looks at the lenticular, adjusting it backwards and forwards. Bruno thinks for half a beat.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Or maybe I’m wrong. You know, it’s a mystery, it’s a mystery that’s why this vision is . Look if I could help anymore I would but, that’s all I know. Good luck. I wish I could’ve seen more. Bruno shuts the door, leaving Mirabel alone in the dark, hallway. She thinks about what he said...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Yeah.... Yeah! Mirabel barges back in!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You “wish you could’ve seen more.” So see more. Have another vision!

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Oh, uh, no, no, see, I don’t, I don’t do visions anymore.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: But ya could.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: But I won’t.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You can’t say “the weight of the world is on your shoulders, the end.” If our fate’s up to me, me says have another vision, maybe it’ll show me what to do --

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Look, even if I wanted to, which I don’t, you wrecked my vision cave, which is a problem ‘cause I need a big open space --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: We’ll find one.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Where?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Use my room. They turn around to see the TAPIR, CHIGUIRO, COATIS... and ANTONIO. He smiles.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: The rats told me everything. Don’t eat those.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Our family needs help. And you need to get outta here. Bruno looks to the rats cleaning themselves with his toothbrush... and then licking the toothbrush.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Mmyeh.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: You should have told me the second you saw the vision! Think of the family. AGUSTÍN I was thinking of my daughter! A GUST of wind blows snow across the room.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Pepa, calm down!

speaker: PEPA
text: I’m doing my best! FÉLIX Yes.

speaker: PEPA
text: You’re lucky it’s not a hurricane!

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mamá, you’ve always been too hard on Mirabel. Small cracks emerge around them.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Look around. We must protect our family, our Encanto. We cannot lose our home! OLD ARTURO ENTERS FROM OUTSIDE ...

speaker: OLD ARTURO
text: Señora, perdón, people in town are becoming anxious about the magic. They want to see you.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Mirabel was in that vision for a reason. Find her. As Abuela leaves, the HOUSE SHAKES... and we carry that tremor into...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: We might wanna hurry. As Bruno prepares a sand circle for his vision...

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Ya can’t hurry the future, uh... Bruno is making his circle of sand , and reaches the Chiguiro sitting in his path.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: And what if I show you something worse? If I see something that you don’t like, you’re gonna be all, “Bruno makes bad things happen, oh, he’s creepy and his vision killed ma’ goldfish.”

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I don’t think you make bad things happen. Sometimes family weirdos just get a bad rap. You can do this. Antonio hands Bruno a stuffed jaguar.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: For the nerves. Antonio smiles at Bruno and nods, like, I believe in you, then heads off. Bruno looks at the stuffed jaguar, then up to Mirabel, considering. Then, with a weighty exhale, Bruno reaches into his ruana and takes out a handful of salt then he throws it behind his back.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I can do this. I can do this. I can do this, I can do this. Bruno lights a match, which he uses to ignite piles of wet leaves. As the smoke begins to spiral around him and Mirabel, Bruno closes his eyes... entering a trance-like state. The magic fills the room and somewhere in the house Bruno’s door starts to glow. On the way to town, Abuela pauses at the strange occurrence, unsure of the cause. The entire room begins to glow in the light of his eyes, wind kicks up, blowing leaves and sand that begins to SWIRL around Mirabel and Bruno, glowing brighter and brighter. Mirabel looks to Bruno who... looks at her and his eyes TWINKLE GREEN, like flecks of glitter.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: You might wanna hang on. He squeezes her hands and energy surrounds them. But we’re back inside as Mirabel gasps with wonder, as WHOOSH! Mirabel is suddenly transported into...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No, I need to know which way it

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: You’re looking at the same thing that I am! If there was something else -- Mirabel, gets an idea... turns the opposite direction. Bruno sees her, thinks it’s pointless. Mirabel looks and... spots a glitter of gold.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Butterfly! Follow the butterfly! As they stand, the BUTTERFLY lands on a REED in a RIVER, Which dissolves as something begins to glow behind it.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Where is that...? The images flutter, Bruno struggles to see it...

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: It’s all outta order -- The image starts to come into focus, it’s... THE CANDLE

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I think you’re gonna help the candle!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: How?! Bruno’s vision moves... showing Mirabel and a GLOWING FIGURE. Who is it? As Mirabel wills herself to bring the figure into focus... we see the shape of a WOMAN.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: There’s someone with you! And you - you fight her!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: What?! Bruno squints trying to interpret what he’s seeing.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Am I fighting or hugging?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Embrace who?! The images grow more and more volatile.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Almost there...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Who is it?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Isabela?! A FLASH OF LIGHT, ENDS THE VISION, leaving us with glittering sand... which falls onto the chiguiro. Bruno looks at the new emerald vision “plate” and smiles.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Oh, your sister, that’s great! Mirabel stares at it in disbelief. Bruno realizes she’s hates his vision. He chucks it like it’s garbage.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Every time.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Why would “embracing Isabela” do anything?

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Idaknow, our family got a miracle, how do you help a family miracle? You hug a sister. Mirabel looks at Bruno like he’s stupid. A noise below draws them to Camilo, who walks across the courtyard. Mirabel and Bruno duck.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Mirabel?! Mirabel?! Camilo’s powers fritz and he gets... a baby head.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Oh, I think we’re running outta time here.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: It’s not gonna work. She won’t hug me, ever, okay? She hates me. Also, I don’t know if you heard, I ruined her proposal. Plus, P.S.--

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: -- Mirabel --

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: -- Mirabel --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: -- and what’s her problem with me anyway, she’s the one with roses coming out her--

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Mirabel. Oh, sorry, sorry, sorry, you see you’re missing the point. The fate of the family is not up to her, it’s up to you. You’re exactly what this family needs. You just have to see it. By yourself, after I leave.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: What? You’re not coming?

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: It was your vision, Mirabel... not mine.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You’re afraid Abuela will see you.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Yep, I mean yes... that too. Hey, after you save the miracle... come visit.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: After I save the miracle, I’m bringing you home. Bruno gives Mirabel a smile then knocks on wood, crosses his fingers, holds his breath and tips backwards into the wall.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: Knock-knock-knock-knock-knock-knock on wood. After he leaves, Mirabel notices candle and magic’s glow becoming more and more erratic. Mirabel looks toward Isabela’s glowing door, psyching herself up.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: You got this. Just gonna save the miracle... with a hug. With that, Mirabel heads to Isa’s door.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Isa? Hey! Mirabel continues through the room.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I know we’ve... had our issues... but I’m... ready to be a better sister... to you... So we should just... hug... Let’s hug it out. Eh? Mirabel looks up to find... Isabela on a crazy flower bed... which looks more over-the-top than usual.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Hug it out...? Luisa can’t lift an empanada. Mariano’s nose looks like a smashed papaya. Have you lost your mind?!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Isa, I feel like you’re upset... and you know what cures being upset? A warm embrace.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Get out. As Mirabel starts to protest, a flower pops out of the vine, covering Mirabel’s mouth.

speaker: ISABELA
text: Everything was perfect! Abuela was happy, the family was happy, you wanna be a better sister, apologize for ruining my life!

speaker: ISABELA
text: Go on... apologize. The room shakes catching Mirabel’s attention.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I... am... sorry... ...That your life is so great!

speaker: ISABELA
text: Out. With a wave of her hand, Isabela’s vines drag Mirabel toward the door. Worried she’ll miss this important opportunity, Mirabel tries to grab onto furniture to stop her momentum.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wait, fine, “I apologize” - I wasn’t trying to ruin your life -- Some of us have bigger problems, you selfish, entitled princess!

speaker: ISABELA
text: Selfish?! I’ve been stuck being perfect my whole entire life and literally the only thing you have ever done for me is mess things up! Mirabel tries to extricate herself from Isabela’s vines as she keeps getting dragged backwards.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Nothing is messed up! You can still marry that big, dumb hunkISABELA

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Oh my gosh... Isa... that is a very serious confession... Okay, get over here, bring it in. But Isa doesn’t respond, she isn’t looking at Mirabel... she’s looking at the ground, at... THAT WEIRD LITTLE CACTUS. Isabela is transfixed. THE HOUSE SHUDDERS.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Isa? Isa picks up the cactus, mesmerized...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Isa, this is the part where we -- Isabela, excited, ignores Mirabel and turns away holding her cactus.

speaker: ISABELA
text: IT’S NOT SYMMETRICAL OR PERFECT BUT IT’S BEAUTIFUL AND IT’S MINE... WHAT ELSE CAN I DO? Mirabel tries to keep up.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Wait! BRING IT IN, BRING IT IN GOOD TALK, BRING IT IN, BRING IT IN LET’S WALK, BRING IT IN, BRING IT

speaker: ISABELA
text: WHAT ELSE CAN I DO?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: FREE HUGS?! BRING IT IN, BRING IT

speaker: ISABELA
text: WHAT COULD I DO IF I JUST GREW WHAT I WAS FEELING IN THE MOMENT?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: DO YOU KNOW WHERE YOU’RE GOING? Isabela’s plants get crazier.

speaker: ISABELA
text: WHAT COULD I DO IF I JUST KNEW IT DIDN’T NEED TO BE PERFECT, IT JUST NEEDED TO BE? AND THEY LET ME BE? Now Isabela is really leaning into it and her plants are going nuts, creating obstacles for Mirabel.

speaker: ISABELA
text: CAN I DELIVER US A RIVER OF SUNDEW? CAREFUL IT’S CARNIVOROUS, A LITTLE JUST WON’T DO...

speaker: ISABELA/MIRABEL
text: HOW FAR CAN YOU/I RISE? THROUGH THE ROOF, TO THE SKIES LET’S GOOO... THEY SMASH THROUGH THE ROOF, AND GO ABOVE THE HOUSE. With Mirabel’s encouragement, Isabela GOES OFF, creating the most amazing combination of wonderful plants we’ve ever seen.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WHOO!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WHAT ELSE, WHAT ELSE?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: SEIZE THE MOMENT, KEEP GOIN! As Mirabel encourages Isabela to express herself with her unique foliage, the plants start emerging in town, smacking Mariano in the face again and rebreaking his nose. Abuela looks at the house horrified, and sees... ISABELA TRANSFORMED and dancing with MIRABEL, WHO EGGS HER ON. Abuela’s eyes go wide.

speaker: ISABELA
text: BUT I’LL STILL BE OKAY...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: HEY, EVERYBODY CLEAR THE WAY, WOOO!

speaker: ISABELA
text: I’M COMING THROUGH WITH TABEBUIA...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: SHE’S COMING THROUGH WITH THAT BOOYAH! The race across the roof, spiraling on Isabela’s wild new plants, a real bond forming between them for the first time.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: YOU’VE CHANGED MINE—

speaker: ISABELA
text: THE WAY IS CLEARER ‘CAUSE YOU’RE HERE AND WELL,

speaker: MIRABEL
text: SHOW ‘EM WHAT YOU CAN DO!!

speaker: ISABELA
text: WHAT ELSE CAN I DO?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: THERE’S NOTHING YOU CAN’T DO—

speaker: ISABELA
text: WHAT ELSE CAN I DO!

speaker: ISABELA
text: You’re a bad influence. Mirabel grins, enjoying the evolution of their relationship.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: What is going on?! Mirabel and Isabela sit up to find... Abuela entering the courtyard... which we reveal looks crazy and dangerous with all of Isabela’s out-of-control plants.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Abuela!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: What are you talking about? Look at our home. Look at your sister!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Please, just - Isabela wasn’t happy and -

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Of course she isn’t happy, you ruined her proposal --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: -- no, no she needed me to ruin her proposal and then we did all of this and the candle burned brighter and the cracks --

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: -- Mirabel --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: That’s why I’m in the vision, I’m saving the miracle!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: You have to stop, Mirabel! A TREMOR shakes the entire ENCANTO. Luisa enters the courtyard, concerned, and stands with Isabela.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: The cracks started with you. Bruno left because of you. Luisa’s losing her powers, Isabela’s out of control, because of you. I don’t know why you weren’t given a gift, but it is not an excuse for you to hurt this family! As Abuela says that cracks emerge all around her. Mirabel’s heart nearly breaks as she looks at Abuela. Julieta and Agustín enter, having heard the argument.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I will never be good enough for you. Will I? No matter how hard I try. Then, a quiet realization as she looks at her family.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No matter how hard any of us tries. Luisa will never be strong enough. Isabela won’t be perfect enough -- Bruno left our family because you only saw the worst in him--

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: -- Bruno didn’t care about this family --

speaker: MIRABEL
text: He loves this family - I love this family! We all love this family! You’re the one that doesn’t care! You’re the one breaking our home! Cracks begins to spread into the town.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Don’t you ever --!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: The miracle is dying, because of you! In that awful moment, a huge cracks rips open between them. As they both stare at each other, stunned, horrified:

speaker: PEPA
text: No, no, no -- FÉLIX The candle! CRACK! A huge crack nearly rips the House in half... snaking all the way up to the CANDLE... which begins to melt at a rapid pace, teetering and in danger of falling into the dark chasm created by the cracks. As everyone tries to get their bearings, Mirabel snaps out of it, and races to save the candle. She calls to the House:

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Casita, get me up there! The house pulls a railing from the balcony for Mirabel to use as a ladder to climb to the roof. Isabela tries to use her vines to reach the candle, but her door fades and her vines dissipate like they were Thanossnapped. The house catches Isabela and breaks her fall. Camilo tries to change his shape to reach the candle, but his door fades and his powers go out. The house catches Camilo and sets him down to safety, but he can’t reach the candle.

speaker: CAMILO
text: No. Mirabel reaches the top of the roof, and looks back to see the MOUNTAINS of the ENCANTO crack open, just like in Bruno’s vision. Nearby, Antonio watches as his animals smash out of his door, which dies and the animals streak off. A chunk of the House falls toward him and Félix narrowly saves him. FÉLIX Careful! Antonio! We gotta get out of here!

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel?! Mirabel! But the House sees the danger too, and starts pushing people out of the House to save them. Desperately using its last bit of power to keep them safe. AGUSTÍN Mirabel! You have to get out!

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel! Bruno runs through space in the walls as the walls close in on him. Bruno saves a rat, puts a bucket on his head and rams through the wall, landing on the grass outside, still unseen by the family. Mirabel looks over the side of the House and sees Bruno is safe and races to the candle. Abuela, gets pushed out the front door, and looks up to see Mirabel clambering across the roof to the candle.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel?! Mirabel leave it!

speaker: PEPA
text: Mirabel! Get out! AGUSTÍN Mirabel! Mirabel strains to reach the sputtering candle... Just as Mirabel finally grabs the candle... Bruno’s tower collapses on the roof. The house slides Mirabel out the way and off the balcony as debris falls down. Mirabel lands, but has no time to escape. In an instant, the House shields Mirabel... saving her life. In the aftermath, Mirabel looks down to see... the candle go out...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: No... Then... a tiny squeak, as the House shutters that just saved her life, wave an injured goodbye... and as Mirabel looks up, devastated, the house... dies.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel...! We begin to hear just the slightest background walla of the family in chaos. As we... PUSH IN ON MIRABEL’S FACE... Julieta comes to check on Mirabel.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel! Are you hurt? Mirabel? Mirabel shakes her head “no, she isn’t hurt.” But she is in shock and reeling.

speaker: PEPA
text: Julieta! Come quick! Julieta!

speaker: JULIETA
text: Don’t move. I’ll be right back! Julieta leaves to go check on the rest of the family, Mirabel can only hear everyone continuing to panic.

speaker: PEPA
text: How could this happen?

speaker: AGUSTIN
text: Everyone okay? Is everyone okay? Mirabel looks over to see Dolores helping Abuela, who is despondent and in shock. As Mirabel watches Abuela... how lost and broken she looks... Mirabel’s chin starts to quiver as the weight of her failure becomes overwhelming. This is Mirabel’s failure and she knows it.

speaker: DOLORES
text: Here, let me help you, let me help you.

speaker: FAMILY WALLA
text: Antonio, don’t cry, papito, don’t cry. / I couldn’t hold it up. / The Encanto is broken. / What do we do now? / My powers ... they’re gone! / What about Antonio, what is he gonna do? We go tighter on Julieta grabbing medicine bottles then turning to find Mirabel, but Mirabel is gone.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel? Mirabel!? Where’s Mirabel? Where is she? Mirabel?! As the family starts to panic, we rack to: the CRACKED MOUNTAINS in the distance behind them... AGUSTÍN MIRABEL! MIRABEL?! As Agustín’s voice rings out, we find:

speaker: LUISA
text: Mirabel! Mirabel! We find the kids from town looking worried.

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: They haven’t found her yet? Juancho and Alejandra shake their heads, no. We find, Julieta and Agustín searching for Mirabel, as well as Félix and Dolores.

speaker: AGUSTIN
text: Mirabel?

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel?! Dolores holds her ear out to see if she can hear Mirabel, but no luck. FÉLIX Mirabel?!

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Mirabel... Reveal the shadow in the woods... was Abuela.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I’m sorry. I didn’t want to... hurt us... I just wanted to... to be something I’m not... As Mirabel starts to cry... Abuela begins to speak... we’ve never heard her voice like this... she’s broken, exhausted... old. She looks to the water.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: I’ve never been able to... come back here... Abuela sits by Mirabel, they look out at the river.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: This river... is where we were given our miracle...

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Where Abuelo Pedro... Mirabel looks at Abuela, trying to understand where she’s coming from... she looks so lost and frail. Abuela looks back to the water...

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: I thought we would have a different life... I thought I would be a different woman... As our music begins... we are transported back in time...

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: I was given a miracle... a second chance... and I was so afraid to lose it... that I lost sight of who our miracle was for. As she passes each new child’s door, years pass and she becomes harder and harder on her family... more rigid... more limiting. Her expectations growing more unhealthy. We move from child to child until... we reach MIRABEL... whose door vanishes. Mirabel looks to Abuela for compassion. And this time, Abuela sees her mistake... but it’s too late...

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: And... I am so sorry... Abuela turns and finds herself... in the rubble of the house. The candle gone. Everything she had hoped for... destroyed. Abuela falls to her knees...

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: ...You never hurt our family, Mirabel. We are broken... Because of me.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Abuela... I can finally see. You lost your home... lost everything... you suffered so much... all alone... so it would never happen again. We were saved because of you. We were given a miracle because of you. We are a family because of you. And nothing could ever be broken, that we can’t fix... together. As this lands on Abuela, her heart seems to crack open. She feels a great weight lift from her shoulders. It is a quiet moment of release fifty year in the making. And suddenly... the SUN PEEKS THROUGH THE CLOUDS, illuminating the river ... beautiful, pink hues, wondrous and spectacular. Alma looks at Mirabel in awe and hugs her tightly, tears in her eyes.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: I asked my Pedro for help. Mirabel... he sent me you. As they embrace in the middle of the river, the butterfly flaps into the wind and soon... thousands of butterflies begin to fill the air, swirling around them. Mirabel tears up, filled with emotion. Abuela looks at the butterflies, this beautiful moment, this place redefined.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: She didn’t do this! She didn’t do this! I gave her a vision! It was me! I was like go! And she was like fttt! She only wanted to help. I... don’t care what you think of me, but if you’re too stubborn to, to -- Abuela Alma embraces and kisses him.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Brunito... Bruno is confused. He looks to Mirabel.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I feel like I missed something important.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Come on... Abuela gives Bruno an affectionate look before taking his hand and walking after Mirabel. Mirabel climbs up on the horse. She helps Abuela who helps Bruno.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: What - what’s happening, where are we going?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Home. As exciting music kicks in, they gallop away, back towards the Encanto.

speaker: PUMPED JUANCHO
text: It’s her, I FOUND HER!

speaker: LITTLE ALEJANDRA
text: I found her!

speaker: INNOCENT CECILIA
text: She’s back, she’s back, she’s back!

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel?! Julieta runs to Mirabel, relieved that her daughter has returned and is okay. She grasps Mirabel in a hug.

speaker: JULIETA
text: Mirabel!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Mamá...

speaker: JULIETA
text: Ay mi amor, I was so worried... we couldn’t find you. AGUSTÍN There were bees everywhere. Mirabel puts her hand on her dad’s welted face. AGUSTÍN I’ll be okay.

speaker: CAMILO
text: Uh... not if we don’t have a house. They look to Camilo. Félix nudges Camilo with his elbow.

speaker: CAMILO
text: What? We don’t have a house, I can’t say we don’t have a house? What is that? Not a house. Mirabel walks up to the standing arch which used to be the front door of the house. She starts singing to the family.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: AND I’M SORRY I HELD ON TOO TIGHT, JUST SO AFRAID I’D LOSE YOU TOO

speaker: ABUELA/MIRABEL
text: THE MIRACLE IS YOU, ALL OF YOU, ALL

speaker: ANTONIO
text: That’s Bruno. Bruno nods, awkward.

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: YEAH, THERE’S A LOT TO SAY ABOUT

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: I GOT A LOTTA ‘POLOGIES I’VE GOT TO

speaker: JULIETA
text: HEY WE’RE JUST HAPPY THAT YOU’RE HERE, OKAY?

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: But --

speaker: PEPA
text: COME INTO THE LIGHT!

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: But --

speaker: AGUSTIN
text: THE TRIPLETS ALL REUNITE!

speaker: JULIETA/PEPA
text: WE’RE GONNA FIND OUR WAY

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: WHAT’S THAT SOUND?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I think it’s everyone in town... The TOWNSPEOPLE appear over the crest of the mountain. We see the TOWN CHILDREN, MARIANO and his Abuela, everyone we’ve met.

speaker: TOWNSPEOPLE
text: HEY! LAY DOWN YOUR LOAD, LAY DOWN YOUR LOAD, WE’RE ONLY DOWN THE ROAD, WE’RE ONLY DOWN THE ROAD, WE

speaker: ISABELA
text: IT’S A DREAM WHEN WE WORK AS A TEAM. YOU’RE SO STRONG.

speaker: LUISA
text: YEAH, BUT SOMETIMES I CRY—

speaker: ISABELA/MIRABEL
text: SO DO I!

speaker: LUISA
text: I MAY NOT BE AS STRONG, BUT I’M GETTING WISER

speaker: ISABELA
text: YEAH, I NEED SUNLIGHT AND

speaker: ISABELA/LUISA
text: STRAIGHT UP TO THE SKY,

speaker: ISABELA/LUISA/MIRABEL/DOLORES
text: LET’S GO... JULIETA and AGUSTÍN watch MIRABEL leading the house building effort with pride.

speaker: JULIETA/MIRABEL
text: THE STARS DON’T SHINE THEY BURN

speaker: JULIETA
text: WOULD YOU WATCH OUR LITTLE GIRL GO? AGUSTÍN

speaker: MIRABEL
text: HEY MARIANO, WHY SO BLUE?

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Y’KNOW, I’VE GOT THIS COUSIN TOO. HAVE YOU MET DOLORES? DOLORES appears out of nowhere.

speaker: DOLORES
text: OKAY I’LL TAKE IT FROM HERE, G’BYE!

speaker: MIRABEL
text: YES!

speaker: TOWNSPEOPLE
text: ALL OF YOU, ALL OF YOU.

speaker: MARIANO
text: LET’S GET MARRIED!

speaker: MIRABEL/ABUELA/TOWNSPEOPLE
text: ALL OF YOU, ALL OF YOU. Smash cut to ABUELA and MIRABEL, standing outside the nearly completed new CASA MADRIGAL. It’s not the same as before... and that’s okay.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: IT ISN’T PERFECT.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: THAT’S TRUE. JUST ONE MORE THING, BEFORE THE

speaker: MIRABEL
text: WHAT?

speaker: LUISA/ISABELA
text: WE SEE HOW BRAVE YOU’VE BEEN... AGUSTÍN/JULIETA

speaker: TIO BRUNO
text: YOU’RE THE REAL GIFT, KID.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: I see... me... All of me. Mirabel looks to her family, who all smile back. Mirabel turns, then places HER doorknob into the FRONT DOOR of the House and... WHOOSH! AN EXPLOSION OF LIGHT races over the entire Encanto, restoring its magic... and redefining the cracks that spread to the entire town. A new miracle. As the House comes back to life once more, the House “waves” at Mirabel and she waves back.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: Hola, Casita. As Mirabel waves, the house zips her through the front doors into the home. Mirabel looks back to her family and the gathering crowd from town.

speaker: MIRABEL
text: C’mon, get in here! The House yanks everyone else into the House too and they cheer and celebrate.

speaker: ABUELA ALMA
text: Everyone, together. The Casita squeezes this whole, imperfectly perfect family together.

speaker: FAMILY... AND MIRABEL
text: La Familia Madrigal!
